differential_diagnoses: Euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis (secondary to SGLT2 inhibitor use)
Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinemia secondary to chronic acetaminophen use)
Acute-on-chronic kidney injury causing toxic metabolite accumulation
next_steps: Obtain arterial blood gas with serum lactate level
Obtain serum anion gap and calculate delta-delta ratio
Perform serum and urine organic acid analysis (to detect 5-oxoproline)
Review current medication list and discontinue dapagliflozin immediately
Maintain hemodynamic support and optimize acid-base status with careful fluid/bicarbonate management if indicated

differential_diagnoses: Euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis (EDKA) secondary to SGLT2 inhibitor use
Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinemia) from chronic acetaminophen and glutathione deficiency
next_steps: Measure arterial blood gas (ABG) with lactate and calculate the anion gap and delta-delta ratio
Obtain serum osmolality and calculate the osmolar gap
Measure serum beta-hydroxybutyrate and urine ketones
Perform urine organic acid profile to detect 5-oxoproline
Continue aggressive hemodynamic support and discontinue dapagliflozin
Consider renal replacement therapy (hemodialysis) if metabolic acidosis is refractory or if metformin toxicity is confirmed
